lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਰੀ ਦੇਖਿ ਵਿਗਾਸੀਅਹਿ ਨਾਲੇ ਹਰਖੁ ਸੁ ਸੋਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: naaree dekh vigaaseeeh naale harakh su sog |
lines.english: Gazing upon his wife, he is pleased. But along with happiness, they bring grief.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਆਪਣੇ ਧੀਆ ਪੂਤ ਸੰਜੋਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh jaanai aapane dheea poot sanjog |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh looks upon his daughters, sons and relatives as his own.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਿਤੁ ਚਲੈ ਵਿਤੁ ਜਾਵਣੋ ਸਾਕਤ ਡੋਲਿ ਡੋਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: chit chalai vit jaavano saakat ddol ddolaae |
lines.english: The consciousness of the wicked, faithless cynics wanders around in search of transitory wealth, unstable and distracted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦਿ ਰੰਗਾਵਲੇ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਭੋਗੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: guramukh sabad rangaavale ahinis har ras bhog |3|
lines.english: The Gurmukhs are attuned to the Word of the Shabad. Day and night, they enjoy the Sublime Essence of the Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਢੂੰਢਿ ਵਿਗੁਚੀਐ ਘਰ ਮਹਿ ਵਸਤੁ ਸੁਥਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: baahar dtoondt vigucheeai ghar meh vasat suthaae |
lines.english: Searching outside of themselves, they are ruined; the object of their search is in that sacred place within the home of the heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਕਰਿ ਮੁਸੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਲੈ ਪਾਇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: manamukh haumai kar musee guramukh palai paae |4|
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs, in their ego, miss it; the Gurmukhs receive it in their laps. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਕਤ ਨਿਰਗੁਣਿਆਰਿਆ ਆਪਣਾ ਮੂਲੁ ਪਛਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: saakat niraguniaariaa aapanaa mool pachhaan |
lines.english: You worthless, faithless cynic-recognize your own origin!

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਕਤੁ ਬਿੰਦੁ ਕਾ ਇਹੁ ਤਨੋ ਅਗਨੀ ਪਾਸਿ ਪਿਰਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: rakat bind kaa ihu tano aganee paas piraan |
lines.english: This body is made of blood and semen. It shall be consigned to the fire in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁਤਾ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਮੰਗੀਐ ਮੁਆ ਨ ਲੋੜੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: bahutaa jeevan mangeeai muaa na lorrai koe |
lines.english: Everyone begs for a long life-no one wishes to die.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਵਣੈ ਕੈ ਵਸਿ ਦੇਹੁਰੀ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਸਚੁ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: pavanai kai vas dehuree masatak sach neesaan |5|
lines.english: The body is under the power of the breath, according to the True Sign inscribed upon your forehead. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮ ਵਿਹੂਣੇ ਕਿਆ ਗਣੀ ਜਿਸੁ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸੁ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: naam vihoone kiaa ganee jis har gur daras na hoe |6|
lines.english: Without the Naam, what good those who do not have the Blessed Vision, the Darshan of the Lord and Guru? ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਜਿਸੁ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵਸਿਆ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: sukh jeevan tis aakheeai jis guramukh vasiaa soe |
lines.english: A life of peace and comfort comes to that Gurmukh, within whom God dwells.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਸੁਪਨੈ ਨਿਸਿ ਭੁਲੀਐ ਜਬ ਲਗਿ ਨਿਦ੍ਰਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: jiau supanai nis bhuleeai jab lag nidraa hoe |
lines.english: In their dreams at night, people wander around as long as they sleep;

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਉ ਸਰਪਨਿ ਕੈ ਵਸਿ ਜੀਅੜਾ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਦੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: eiau sarapan kai vas jeearraa antar haumai doe |
lines.english: just so, they are under the power of the snake Maya, as long as their hearts are filled with ego and duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਨਿ ਮਰੈ ਜਲੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਜਿਉ ਬਾਰਿਕ ਦੂਧੈ ਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: agan marai jal paaeeai jiau baarik doodhai maae |
lines.english: As thirst is quenched with water, and the baby is satisfied with mother's milk,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਹੋਇ ਵੀਚਾਰੀਐ ਸੁਪਨਾ ਇਹੁ ਜਗੁ ਲੋਇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: guramat hoe veechaareeai supanaa ihu jag loe |7|
lines.english: Through the Guru's Teachings, they come to understand and see that this world is just a dream. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਰਸਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਜੀਵਾ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਇ ॥੮॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh har ras milai jeevaa har gun gaae |8|15|
lines.english: -O Nanak, so does the Gurmukh live, receiving the Sublime Essence of the Lord, and singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||8||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਜਲ ਕਮਲ ਸੁ ਨਾ ਥੀਐ ਬਿਨੁ ਜਲ ਮੀਨੁ ਮਰਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin jal kamal su naa theeai bin jal meen maraae |
lines.english: and as the lotus does not exist without water, and as the fish dies without water

lines.gurmukhi: ਡੂੰਗਰੁ ਦੇਖਿ ਡਰਾਵਣੋ ਪੇਈਅੜੈ ਡਰੀਆਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: ddoongar dekh ddaraavano peeearrai ddareeaas |
lines.english: Beholding the terrifying mountain in this world of my father's home, I am terrified.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰੀਰਾਗੁ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: sireeraag mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Siree Raag, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਈ ਰੇ ਭਵਜਲੁ ਬਿਖਮੁ ਡਰਾਂਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhaaee re bhavajal bikham ddaraanau |
lines.english: O Siblings of Destiny, the terrifying world-ocean is so difficult to cross-I am terrified!

lines.gurmukhi: ਊਚਉ ਪਰਬਤੁ ਗਾਖੜੋ ਨਾ ਪਉੜੀ ਤਿਤੁ ਤਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: aoochau parabat gaakharro naa paurree tith taas |
lines.english: It is so difficult to climb this high mountain; there is no ladder which reaches up there.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਜਾਣਿਆ ਗੁਰਿ ਮੇਲੀ ਤਰੀਆਸੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: guramukh antar jaaniaa gur melee tareeaas |1|
lines.english: But as Gurmukh, I know that it is within my self; the Guru has brought me to Union, and so I cross over. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਲਾ ਚਲਾ ਜੇ ਕਰੀ ਜਾਣਾ ਚਲਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: chalaa chalaa je karee jaanaa chalanahaar |
lines.english: I may say, "I am going, I am going", but I know that, in the end, I must really go.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਰਸਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਗੁਰੁ ਤਾਰੇ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: pooraa satigur ras milai gur taare har naau |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Perfect True Guru, in His Pleasure, has met with me; the Guru has saved me, through the Name of the Lord. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੀ ਸਚਾ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਾ ਸਚੈ ਥਾਨਿ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bhee sachaa saalaahanaa sachai thaan piaar |2|
lines.english: So praise the True One continually, and love His Place of Truth. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਆਇਆ ਸੋ ਚਲਸੀ ਅਮਰੁ ਸੁ ਗੁਰੁ ਕਰਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jo aaeaa so chalasee amar su gur karataar |
lines.english: Whoever comes must also go. Only the Guru and the Creator are Eternal.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਸ ਹੀ ਨਾਲਿ ਨ ਚਲਿਆ ਖਪਿ ਖਪਿ ਮੁਏ ਅਸਾਰ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: kis hee naal na chaliaa khap khap mue asaar |3|
lines.english: -none of these will go along with anyone in the end, and yet, the fools bother themselves to exhaustion with these, and then die. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਸਤੀ ਘੋੜੇ ਪਾਖਰੇ ਲਸਕਰ ਲਖ ਅਪਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: hasatee ghorre paakhare lasakar lakh apaar |
lines.english: elephants, saddled horses, hundreds of thousands of uncounted armies

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰ ਘਰ ਮਹਲਾ ਸੋਹਣੇ ਪਕੇ ਕੋਟ ਹਜਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: dar ghar mahalaa sohane pake kott hajaar |
lines.english: Beautiful gates, houses and palaces, solidly built forts,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਇਨਾ ਰੁਪਾ ਸੰਚੀਐ ਮਾਲੁ ਜਾਲੁ ਜੰਜਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: sueinaa rupaa sancheeai maal jaal janjaal |
lines.english: You may gather gold and sliver, but wealth is just a net of entanglement.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਜਗ ਮਹਿ ਦੋਹੀ ਫੇਰੀਐ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਸਿਰਿ ਕਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: sabh jag meh dohee fereeai bin naavai sir kaal |
lines.english: You may beat the drum and proclaim authority over the whole world, but without the Name, death hovers over your head.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿੰਡੁ ਪੜੈ ਜੀਉ ਖੇਲਸੀ ਬਦਫੈਲੀ ਕਿਆ ਹਾਲੁ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: pindd parrai jeeo khelasee badafailee kiaa haal |4|
lines.english: When the body falls, the play of life is over; what shall be the condition of the evil-doers then? ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤਾ ਦੇਖਿ ਵਿਗਸੀਐ ਨਾਰੀ ਸੇਜ ਭਤਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: putaa dekh vigaseeai naaree sej bhataar |
lines.english: The husband is delighted seeing his sons, and his wife upon his bed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੇਹੂ ਖੇਹ ਰਲਾਈਐ ਛੋਡਿ ਚਲੈ ਘਰ ਬਾਰੁ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: khehoo kheh ralaaeeai chhodd chalai ghar baar |5|
lines.english: But dust shall mix with dust, and he shall depart, leaving hearth and home behind. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੋਆ ਚੰਦਨੁ ਲਾਈਐ ਕਾਪੜੁ ਰੂਪੁ ਸੀਗਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: choaa chandan laaeeai kaaparr roop seegaar |
lines.english: He applies sandalwood and scented oils, and dresses himself in his beautiful clothes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਰ ਮਲੂਕ ਕਹਾਈਐ ਰਾਜਾ ਰਾਉ ਕਿ ਖਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: mehar malook kahaaeeai raajaa raau ki khaan |
lines.english: He may be called a chief, an emperor, a king, a governor or a lord;

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਉਧਰੀ ਰਾਉ ਸਦਾਈਐ ਜਲਿ ਬਲੀਐ ਅਭਿਮਾਨ ॥
lines.translit: chaudharee raau sadaaeeai jal baleeai abhimaan |
lines.english: he may present himself as a leader or a chief, but this just burns him in the fire of egotistical pride.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਇਸੀ ਜੋ ਆਇਆ ਜਗ ਮਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: haumai kar kar jaaeisee jo aaeaa jag maeh |
lines.english: Whoever comes into the world and indulges in ego, must depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ਜਿਉ ਡਵਿ ਦਧਾ ਕਾਨੁ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: manamukh naam visaariaa jiau ddav dadhaa kaan |6|
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh has forgotten the Naam. He is like straw, burning in the forest fire. ||6||
